summary: FAIL. The manuscript delivers strong causal consequence, concrete economic pressure, and sustained past-tense control across all chapters, but it does not yet evidence shortlist-level voice distinction and sentence-level freshness. Cross-chapter dialogue register repeatedly collapses into the same procedural boundary lexicon, and several key chapters over-index on memo-like exposition instead of dramatized scene pressure. Continuity/timeline checks are broadly stable; primary blockers are craft-level voice homogenization and recurring prose formulas.

findings.problem: High-pressure dialogue defaults to shared procedural diction ("dates and amounts", "say the number", "something is not an amount"), flattening idiolect separation between Nora, Eli, and June and creating a scripted register drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2172, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2192, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2266, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2292 so Nora keeps ICU-triage precision while June keeps younger, socially compressed campus phrasing; remove repeated command-template phrasing and add interruptions/false starts unique to each speaker.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2172 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2266, a reader can identify Nora vs June without dialogue tags in at least two consecutive exchanges; FAIL if both voices still rely on the same amount/proof command syntax.

findings.problem: Marisol's confrontation/intimacy register converges toward the same coaching-contract cadence used by Nora/Theo ("meeting voice", "do you hear me", "give me a date"), weakening idiolect distinctness in a chapter where voice differentiation should peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3433, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3443, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3721, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3725 to preserve Marisol's sensory/plainspoken service-work idiom and reduce explicit coaching lines; shift one pressure beat from declarative instruction into action/silence.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised Marisol lines at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3433 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3721 are recognizably distinct from Nora's legal cadence and carry pressure without repeating "say it plain / I hear you" loops; FAIL if dialogue still reads as interchangeable boundary coaching.

findings.problem: Style-bible token overfitting creates mantra-like diction ("specifics, deadlines, math, proof, facts, no narrative") that flattens tonal range and makes Nora/Marisol/Eli share one procedural voice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and vary the rhetorical pattern around snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3967, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3977, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3993, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3999; keep legal/financial data but render one beat through embodied interruption rather than another command sentence.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3967 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3993 preserve all key dates/accounts while eliminating repeated "facts/proof/amount" phrasing in back-to-back lines; FAIL if the same token cluster still drives both voices.

findings.problem: Reentry chapter plays as a policy hearing for too long; Nora and June both speak in near-identical contractual syntax, muting scene volatility and idiolect separation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain legal terms but retexture dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5690, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5696, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5716, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5816, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5842 with more overlap, interruption, and character-specific wording (Nora clinical deadlines; June sharper campus irony; Eli fractured shame responses).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised scenes anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5690 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5816 allow a reader to identify speaker by rhythm/lexicon alone in at least three exchanges; FAIL if the conversation still reads as uniform legal-process dialogue.

findings.problem: Climactic father-daughter boundary scene is over-governed by checklist cadence, so June's voice repeatedly collapses into the same structured compliance register used elsewhere in the book.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep boundary clarity but loosen cadence at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6491, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6495, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6503, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6515 by adding tonal swings, partial sentences, and one emotionally messy interruption that still preserves terms.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6491 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6515 preserve all boundaries but include at least one abrupt tonal shift and one non-template interruption from June; FAIL if the exchange remains a clean rule recital.

findings.problem: Procedural exposition crowds out dramatized consequence: the repayment block reads as memo text rather than scene pressure, reducing narrative voltage at a pivotal turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Refactor snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4079, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4083, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4091, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4097 so at least one listed commitment is enacted immediately in-scene (attempt, obstruction, and cost), with the remaining plan compressed.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4079 includes one immediate irreversible action and one concrete cost paid in that same sequence; FAIL if the section remains primarily a forward-looking bullet ledger.

findings.problem: Institutional call/paperwork sequence runs long in transcript mode, delaying the chapter's decisive pivot and muting embodied stakes despite high-pressure content.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten repetitive disclosure language at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4246, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4258, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4262, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4272; pull the send-now vs travel-now decision beat earlier and stage it physically before the depot accounting list at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4334.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines around snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4246 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4334 deliver a clear, on-page pivot choice with immediate sacrifice; FAIL if the scene still reads as extended call-center transcript before choice.

findings.problem: Recurring full-book formula metaphor pattern appears in 8 chapters (chapter_05, chapter_09, chapter_10, chapter_11, chapter_12, chapter_14, chapter_15, chapter_16): abstract dialogue units repeatedly "land harder" as impact shorthand. This chapter has two instances (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1387, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1477). Chapter action: REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1387 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1477, replace the "landed" impact template with chapter-specific sensory or behavioral reactions tied to Theo's history in this room; do not add new "landed harder" variants elsewhere in this chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if neither targeted line retains the "landed harder/landed exactly" construction and the revised reaction is concrete to this kitchen scene; FAIL if the chapter still uses the same impact-template wording at either cited line.

findings.problem: Same full-book recurring formula metaphor pattern (8-chapter spread) appears here as "The number landed..." impact shorthand. Chapter action: REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2895 so the billing shock is rendered through fresh physical cognition (breath, posture, motor decision) instead of "landed" impact wording.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2895 conveys shock without any "landed" formula; FAIL if the same abstract-impact template remains.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3308 no longer uses "landed" impact phrasing and still preserves the emotional beat; FAIL if only synonyms of the same template are swapped in.

findings.problem: Same 8-chapter recurring "landed harder" formula appears here. This is the strongest/earliest high-stakes intimate-confrontation usage among the affected set. Chapter action: KEEP.
findings.rewrite_direction: KEEP the instance at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3439 as one of at most 1-2 retained motif uses in the novel; during revision of surrounding lines, avoid introducing additional "landed" impact variants in this chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3439 remains unchanged in principle and no new "landed harder" formula appears elsewhere in chapter_11; FAIL if this chapter adds or duplicates the same impact template.

findings.problem: Same 8-chapter recurring "landed harder" formula appears multiple times in this chapter (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4031, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4143), creating local echo and reducing rhetorical freshness. Chapter action: REPLACE.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace both formula instances at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4031 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4143 with distinct reactions (one externalized action, one interior sensory detail) that do not reuse the impact template.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4841 communicates the blow without "landed" formula language; FAIL if the same impact shorthand persists.
